channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Police in the Danish capital said two predawn explosions took place Wednesday in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting the nearby Jewish school to close for the day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK leader Starmer is on a 1-day visit to EU headquarters hoping to heal ties battered by Brexit
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is in Brussels on a mission to try to improve tattered ties with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank risk crossing Israel's separation barrier to flee failing economy
item.description: YATTA, West Bank (AP) — At dawn in mid-May, Sayyed Ayyed and dozens of other unemployed Palestinian men gathered at the foot of the towering wall of concrete and barbed wire dividing the occupied West Bank from Israel. A smuggler was there with a ladder and ropes.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai police arrest driver and work to identify victims of school bus fire that killed 23
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai police arrested the driver of a bus carrying young students and teachers that caught fire and killed 23 in suburban Bangkok, as families arrived in the capital Wednesday to help identify their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum will keep doing mañanera briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President, Claudia Sheinbaum, will start her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning press briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaza health officials say death toll from a large Israeli operation has climbed to 51
item.description: Gaza’s Health Ministry says at least 51 people have been killed and another 82 wounded in a large Israeli air and ground operation in the southern city of Khan Younis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of rapidly escalating attacks between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer to a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, AAMER MADHANI, and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: A plane carrying Brazil President Lula lands safely at Mexico City airport after circling for hours
item.description: ZUMPANGO, Mexico (AP) — An airplane carrying Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva back to Brasilia landed safely on Tuesday at Mexico City’s Felipe Angeles airport after circling for hours due to a technical problem.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and MARTIN SILVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran attacks ratchet up Middle East conflict: 5 takeaways
item.description: The Middle East is a powder keg after Iran launched nearly 200 missiles toward Israel Wednesday, with the United States vowing "severe consequences" for the action. No deaths in Israel were immediately assessed, and U.S. officials said the missiles — aimed at Israel and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: How does Israel’s Iron Dome work?
item.description: Iran launched missiles toward Israel Tuesday, just days after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in a strike on Lebanon. The Iranian blitz comes after a nearly yearlong cross-border conflict between Hezbollah, a star proxy group for Tehran, and Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: The five smallest countries in the world cover less than 50 square miles combined
item.description: Vatican City, Monaco, Nauru, Tuvalu and San Marino make up the world's smallest countries. The biggest of the group, San Marino, is only 25 square miles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran launches missile attack on Israel
item.description: Iran launched a barrage of missiles at Israel on Tuesday, shortly after the U.S. warned an attack was imminent. The attack appeared to be over within an hour of starting.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Russian fighter jet flies within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The U.S. military is slamming Russia for an “unsafe” encounter in the skies off the coast of Alaska after footage released Monday shows a Kremlin fighter jet cutting off an Air Force F-16. The incident, which took place Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels are looking to gain from continuing conflict in the Middle East
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — In the days since Israel intensified its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon, including the strike that killed the militant group's leader Hassan Nasrallah, Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels have been quick to show they are an important player in the complex conflicts convulsing the M
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles at Israel, though Israeli authorities said there were no injuries and scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea unveils its most powerful missile, which could reach North Korea's underground bunkers
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea unveiled its most powerful ballistic missile and other weapons that could target North Korea during a massive Armed Forces Day ceremony on Tuesday, as the South's president warned the North's regime would collapse if it attempts to use nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A cruise ship bound on a round-the-world voyage leaves Belfast after a 4-month delay
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cruise ship taking passengers on a 3 ½-year around-the-world voyage has finally sailed from Belfast's dockside, after spending four months stuck in the Northern Ireland capital.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tibetan group in India protests against China and human rights situation in homeland
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Dozens of Tibetans living in India protested outside China's embassy on Tuesday against the human rights situation in their homeland, which China annexed in 1951.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum has been sworn in as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Megan Janetsky And Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli ministers frustrated over US, IDF leak on Lebanon operation: Report
item.description: Israeli ministers agree to operational strategy for ground invasion hours after officials in the US confirmed a 'limited' invasion of Lebanon was coming, drawing frustration from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Death toll rises to 18 in South Africa mass shootings, police say
item.description: The death toll from mass shootings at two houses on the same street in a South African village over the weekend has risen to 18, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's ground invasion into Lebanon imminent as cabinet approves next phase of the war
item.description: President Biden demanded a 'ceasefire now' following reports that Washington had been briefed by Israel on its plans to launch a 'limited' ground invasion into Lebanon imminently in fight with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah says it will choose Nasrallah's successor 'at the earliest opportunity'
item.description: Hezbollah’s second-in-command, deputy leader Naim Qassem, says the terrorist group will choose its new leader "at the earliest opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hamas commander in Lebanon, 3 Palestinian militant leaders killed in separate strike
item.description: Fateh Sherif, the head of the Lebanon branch of the Palestinian terrorist group Hamas, has been killed overnight in an airstrike, Israeli officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah's acting leader vows to fight on after Nasrallah's death
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah's acting leader vowed Monday to continue battling Israel and said the militant group was prepared for a long fight even after much of its top command was wiped out, including its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 148 are dead in floods and landslides in Nepal
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Rescuers in Nepal recovered dozens of bodies from buses and other vehicles that were buried in landslides near the capital Kathmandu, as the death toll from flooding rose to at least 148 with dozens missing, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuanian FM warns Russia can do 'so much damage to its neighbors'
item.description: Lithuania has been one of the most vocal opponents of Russia since Moscow's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The NATO country has raised its defense spending to over 3.2% annually.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli Air Force strikes Houthi targets in Yemen with 'extensive' operation
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said the Israeli Air Force struck Houthi terror targets, including power plants and a seaport used to import oil, in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European officials dismiss claim world leaders 'are laughing' at Trump, praise his 'strong message'
item.description: Vice President Harris, during September's presidential debate, claimed world leaders were "laughing" at former President Trump. Several foreign officials beg to differ.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy meet in New York as election holds high stakes for US support for Ukraine
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met face-to-face with Donald Trump on Friday with public tensions rising between the two over Ukraine's defense against Russia's invasion and in the midst of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian missiles and drones target Kyiv for 5 hours and hit Ukraine's power grid
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s air defenses battled an overnight Russian aerial attack on the capital Kyiv for five hours, officials said Thursday, as missiles and drones again hammered the Ukrainian power grid. The Kyiv attack injured at least two people, Ukraine’s Emergency Service said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation
